data.title: Consumer sentiment roughly stable with initial September reading; inflation expectations tick down
data.description:
U.S. Consumer Sentiment Index: 55.1 in September, revised from 55.4 in prior estimate, and 58.2 in August, according to data released by the University of Michigan on Friday.

One-year implied inflation: +4.7% vs. +4.8% prior estimate and +4.8% in August.

Five-year inflation expectations: +3.7% vs. prior September reading of +3.9% and +3.5% in August.

Current conditions index: 60.4 vs. previous estimate of 61.2 and 61.7 in prior month.

Consumer expectations: 51.7 vs. 51.8 prior estimate and 55.9 in August.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Crypto exchange Kraken closes $500M fundraise, valuing it at $15B - report
data.description:
Kraken has closed a $500M funding round, valuing the crypto exchange at $15B in the run-up to an expected initial public offering, according to a recent media report.

The fundraise, which closed this month, did not feature a primary investor, Fortune reported, citing a person familiar with the matter. The terms, including the valuation, were set by Kraken.

Kraken co-CEO Arjun Sethi, along with investment managers and venture capitalists, were among the backers in the round, the person added.

Until this year, Kraken was said to have raised only $27M in venture capital funding, the article said, noting the crypto platform raked in nearly $80M in post-EBITDA earnings in Q2.

Like many of its rivals, the company has made a number of pro-growth moves this year, from launching crypto derivatives trading in the U.S. to acquiring Israel-based automation company Capitalise.ai, amid a friendlier regulatory environment under the Trump administration.

Kraken declined to comment to Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Brandywine Realty Trust cuts dividend by almost half, estimates Q4 earnings charge
data.description:
• Brandywine Realty Trust (NYSE:BDN) declared $0.08/share quarterly dividend, -46.7% decrease from prior dividend of $0.15.
  • Forward yield 7.24%
  • Payable Oct. 23; for shareholders of record Oct. 9; ex-div Oct. 9.
  • "During October, we intend to prepay a secured loan using a combination of cash on hand, our $600 million unsecured line-of-credit and proceeds from additional sources of liquidity, as appropriate," said the REIT in a September 26 statement.
  • "The secured loan totals $245 million and has a scheduled maturity date in February 2028 and is currently secured by 7 properties totaling 1.4 million square feet located in several of our segments. If the secured loan is prepaid, Brandywine will have a wholly owned operating portfolio that is fully unencumbered," added the statement.
  • "We estimate the prepayment will result in a fourth quarter earnings charge totaling $12-$14 million, or $0.07-$0.09 per common share which is currently not included in our 2025 earnings guidance," said the statement.
  • Shares were -1.56% during Friday morning trade to $4.42.
  • See BDN Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: First Merchants downgraded at Raymond James post First Savings acquisition announcement
data.description:
First Merchants (NASDAQ:FRME) was downgraded at Raymond James & Associates following the financial holding company's announcement of First Savings Financial Group (NASDAQ:FSFG) acquisition.

First Merchants said yesterday that it has entered into a definitive merger agreement to acquire FSFG in an all-stock transaction valued at ~$241.3M.

"Though the deal creates entry into the Louisville MSA, boosts EPS forecasts, and was executed at a fair market price (1.35x P/TBV), the use of capital lessens the likelihood of share repurchases and material securities restructurings near-term, while its core NIM path faces some headwinds with lower rates," said analyst Daniel Tamayo.

"Finally, the acquired specialty business lines from First Savings offer no/minimal funding sources and could create incremental funding headwinds at a time when organic lending opportunities appear strong, driving our belief the deal could delay its path towards core financial improvement," said the analyst in a research note.

"With several quarters necessary to prove out the merits of the deal along with NIM headwinds from lower rates despite forecasts for solid profitability, growth and credit metrics to continue, we see fewer near-term catalysts for shares to outperform, trading mostly in-line with peers," said the note.

Raymond James cut its recommendation on FRME to Market Perform from Outperform, and removed the $45.00 price target on the stock.

The rating on the stock aligns with Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating of Hold. Meanwhile, SA authors as well as the sell-side analysts grade the stock as Buy.

data.title: Barkin sees 'shaky' ground on Fed's dual mandate, but path ahead looks 'stable'
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond President Tom Barkin said Friday "the ground may look shaky" for the U.S. central bank, with inflation still above target and downward pressure on employment, though the path ahead looks "stable" if the Fed stays focused on balance.

"Since 2022, with inflation above our target and the labor market healthy, it made sense for us to focus our attention on inflation," Barkin said in a prepared speech at the Peterson Institute for International Economics.

"Now, with inflation risks still elevated but the downside risks to employment rising, we face a more challenging situation," he said. "We are at the top of the telephone pole, if you will. With our two mandate goals in tension, our focus is more about balance."

When looking further ahead, though, there are grounds for optimism on both inflation and employment.

On the jobs side, he noted that employment growth has retreated markedly, while the unemployment rate remained relatively stable. "There may be fewer jobs to fill, but there are also fewer people vying for each job," he said.

Barkin added that labor "demand remains solid and could even get a boost from stimulus embedded in the recent tax bill."

He contended that upside risks to inflation should also be limited, with businesses facing consumer pushback to passing on higher import costs from tariffs.

The 25-basis-point rate cut at the Federal Open Market Committee meeting last week "should help support the labor market while maintaining pressure on inflation, in the interest of keeping the economy on path to that more stable horizon," the Richmond Fed chief said.

data.title: Xpeng increases expansion into new European markets
data.description:
Shares of Xpeng (NYSE:XPEV) are getting a lift at the open after the company said it will increase its exposure to the European market through partnerships with regional dealerships.

As part of a broader strategy to increase competition with its Chinese rivals and build its overseas markets to contribute 50% to sales within the next 10 years, Xpeng (NYSE:XPEV) will partner with Switzerland’s Hedin Group to launch the 2025 G6 and G9 SUV in the country, expanding into Austria, Hungary, Slovenia, and Croatia. The operations in Hungary, Slovenia, and Croatia will be managed through a joint venture with AutoWallis Group and Salvador Caetano Group.

The move further into Europe intends to mirror Xpeng’s (NYSE:XPEV) successful integration in Germany where the company operates 35 sales outlets across the country.

Between January and August, Xpeng (XPEV) delivered nearly 25K vehicles overseas, an increase of 137% from the same period last year. The majority of these exports land in Europe and Southeast Asia with Norway, Sweden, and Thailand the company’s largest overseas markets.

data.title: Preorders for ROG Xbox Ally X handheld gaming PC sold out on the Xbox Store
data.description:
Microsoft's gaming division, Xbox (NASDAQ:MSFT), said Friday that preorders for the upcoming flagship handheld gaming PC, made in collaboration with Taiwanese computer giant ASUS (OTCPK:ASUUY), are sold out on its website worldwide just one day after the announcement.

Xbox said in a post on social media platform X that preorders for ROG Xbox Ally X are sold out globally on the Xbox Store. Orders can still be placed via the ASUS store online or through a local retailer.

The flagship ROG Xbox Ally X, which is retailing for $1,000, boasts the new AMD Ryzen AI Z2 Extreme Processor, while the standard ROG Xbox Ally, priced at $600, is powered by an AMD Ryzen Z2 A Processor.

Xbox and ASUS opened preorders late on Thursday for two variants of the gaming device, which are direct competitors to Nintendo's (OTCPK:NTDOY) Switch 2 and Sony's (SONY) PlayStation Portal.

data.title: AstraZeneca becomes latest drugmaker to launch direct-to-consumer platform
data.description:
AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) on Friday became the latest leading drugmaker to roll out a direct-to-consumer platform in the U.S. with the launch of AstraZeneca Direct, through which the company aims to offer some of its brand-name pharmaceuticals at a sharp discount to cash-paying customers.

From Oct. 1, Astra's (NASDAQ:AZN) asthma drug Airsupra and diabetes therapy Farxiga will be available through AstraZeneca Direct for direct purchases and home delivery, the Anglo-Swedish drugmaker said, adding that their cash prices will be up to 70% lower than their list prices.

“The program complements our existing patient support services and is an important step forward in offering patients the medication they need, when and how they need it,” the company's U.S. Country President Joris Silon remarked.

The launch comes days ahead of a Sept. 29 deadline issued by the White House to drugmakers, including Astra (NASDAQ:AZN), to lower U.S. drug prices in line with President Donald Trump’s most-favored-nation policy. AstraZeneca’s (AZN) Big Pharma rivals, such as Eli Lilly (LLY), Novo Nordisk (NVO), and Pfizer (PFE), already operate DTC platforms.

data.title: Wall Street opens higher as August PCE index comes inline
data.description:
The stock market traded higher on Friday, as the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge came inline.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.4%, the Dow (DJI) +0.5%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.18%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.65%.

The Core Personal Consumption Expenditure Price Index (excludes food and energy) came in at +0.2% vs. +0.2% consensus and +0.2% prior (revised from +0.3%), and +2.9% Y/Y vs. +2.9% consensus and +2.9% in July.

The PCE Price Index came in at +0.3% vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, and +2.7% Y/Y vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior.

Wall Street finished lower in a roller coaster session on Thursday as the broader market averages observed selling pressure after jobless claims fell and GDP was revised higher in the third estimate.

“Markets continued to struggle yesterday, with a broad-based selloff that saw the S&P 500 post a third consecutive decline. The main catalyst was a strong batch of U.S. data, which meant investors dialed back their expectations for rapid Fed rate cuts and pushed front-end Treasury yields higher,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. 

That U.S. data was the big market driver yesterday, as it painted a more resilient economic picture than previously thought, which undercut some calls for faster rate cuts, Allen said.  

The September consumer sentiment will be released during market hours.

Forecasters see no revision from the preliminary readings, with the sentiment index unchanged at 55.4 and inflation expectations unrevised at 4.8%.

On tariff news, President Donald Trump announced sweeping new tariffs, including 100% on branded and patented pharmaceutical drugs, to take effect on October 1, with an exemption if the company is actively building a new manufacturing facility in the U.S.

“The 100% pharmaceutical tariff applies only to branded drugs, and constructing a factory may lead to an exemption. Many pharmaceutical companies have facilities in the U.S., so it may be relatively easy to superficially expand those facilities to avoid tariffs being applied,” UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: Citi, J.P. Morgan upgrades Riot; IREN, CleanSpark downgraded among bitcoin miners
data.description:
Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) stock was surging as Citi Research and J.P. Morgan upgraded the bitcoin miner, with the latter acting on other major bitcoin mining stocks as well.

Shares were +5.32% Friday pre-market to $17.63.

"Recent HPC/AI activity, including Fluidstack with WULF/CIPH and notably, Oracle's $300B infrastructure partnership with OpenAI has caused us to rethink timing, as well as the value of RIOT's planned capacity for HPC/AI," said Citi analyst Peter Christiansen in a research note.

"We await end-of-month BTC metrics to finalize our 3Q estimates, though make changes to our SOTP framework, now assuming 400 MWs of approved/energized capacity valued at $12.4M/MW. We partially offset this increase, assuming a 15%-20% lower EBITDA multiple for BTC mining given rising momentum in the BTC network hashrate," said the research note.

Citi raised its recommendation on RIOT to Buy/High Risk from Neutral, and increased the price target to $24.00 from $13.75.

Meanwhile, J.P. Morgan analysts Reginald Smith and Charles Pearce upgraded Riot to Overweight from Neutral, and increased the price target to $19.00 from $15.00, citing that the stock offers the best value today, among bitcoin miners.

"We apply a 50% probability that Cipher (CIFR), IREN (NASDAQ:IREN) and Riot sign HPC colocation deals at remaining near-term capacity they are shopping, using Core Scientific's ~800 MWs of committed capacity to CoreWeave as an upper limit on near-term demand from a single tenant," said J.P. Morgan.

"Under this framework, we see the most relative upside at Riot (+14%)," said J.P. Morgan analysts.

"We estimate IREN shares are pricing in a >1 GW colocation agreement at Sweetwater, which would be a deal of record scale and capex (>$10bn), which could be possible over time, but creates more downside risk than upside potential at current levels, in our view," said the investment bank in a research note.

IREN was cut to Underweight from Neutral, but the price target was increased to $24.00 from $16.00. Shares were -4.23% pre-market to $44.33.

Meanwhile, CleanSpark (NASDAQ:CLSK) was downgraded to Neutral from Overweight, and the price target reduced to $14.00 from $15.00. Shares were -3.87% to $13.15.

"CLSK shares seem to fully price in the company's recent expansion to 50 EH/s, and investors will likely need more clarity around the company's latest thinking on HPC to drive further upside from current levels," said Smith and Pearce in the note.

Cipher Mining (CIFR) was rated Neutral with a price target of $12.00, while MARA Holdings (MARA) was graded Overweight with a price target of $20.00.

MARA was 1.31% higher to $16.28, while CIFR was 0.39% lower to $11.62.
